name: Fiddlesticks

basic_attack.calculation_notes: Missile speed uses canonical ranged fallback (2000) because source champion payload omits basic-attack missile speed.

passive.name: A Harmless Scarecrow
passive.description_source: Innate:  Fiddlesticks has a  Scarecrow Effigy in place of a trinket, which will imitate Fiddlesticks. Fiddlesticks can impersonate an effigy by remaining idle out of combat.

effects.description: Innate - Scarecrow Effigy: Fiddlesticks begins the game with an exclusive Scarecrow Effigy, which permanently occupies the trinket slot. Fiddlesticks can pretend to be an Effigy by standing still and not acting nor being acted upon for 2 seconds, during which it hides its health bar, adjusts its body into a scarecrow and extends its arm out to expose its lantern.
Innate - A Harmless Scarecrow: From level 6 onward, placing an Effigy also summons a Sweeper Drone at the location for 6 seconds.

passive.description: Innate - Scarecrow Effigy: Fiddlesticks begins the game with an exclusive Scarecrow Effigy, which permanently occupies the trinket slot. Fiddlesticks can pretend to be an Effigy by standing still and not acting nor being acted upon for 2 seconds, during which it hides its health bar, adjusts its body into a scarecrow and extends its arm out to expose its lantern.
context_notes: Source notes (summary): Scarecrow Effigies are unavailable on Howling Abyss until Fiddlesticks uses or sells its Poro-Snax.

basic_ability_1.name: Terrify
basic_ability_1.description_source: Passive: While  Fiddlesticks is unseen or impersonating an  effigy, its next damaging ability will briefly  fear targets hit.

effects.context_notes: Passive: While Fiddlesticks has been out of combat for at least 2.5 seconds and is not visible to the enemy team (or while it is impersonating an Effigy), its next damaging ability additionally fears targets hit.
Active: Fiddlesticks launches a crow at the target enemy that resolves after the cast delay, dealing magic damage and applying fear unless the target is currently fear-immune.
Active: Fiddlesticks launches a crow at the target enemy that resolves after the cast delay, dealing magic damage and applying fear unless the target is currently fear-immune.
Active: Fiddlesticks launches a crow at the target enemy that resolves after the cast delay, dealing magic damage and applying fear unless the target is currently fear-immune.
Targets feared by Fiddlesticks by any means cannot be affected by it again for a duration equal to Terrify's cooldown.
Targets feared by Fiddlesticks by any means cannot be affected by it again for a duration equal to Terrify's cooldown.

basic_ability_1.description: Passive: While Fiddlesticks has been out of combat for at least 2.5 seconds and is not visible to the enemy team, or is pretending to be an Effigy, its next damaging ability will additionally fear targets hit for a duration.
context_notes: Source notes (summary): The fear-immunity timer is not refreshed by subsequent attempts to fear the target.
Manual review (2026-02-24): Terrify is target-required and non-instant; the active cast resolves after windup and then applies damage/fear, while passive fear-gating depends on unseen/effigy state.
Manual review (2026-02-24): Fear-immunity handling is branch-sensitive (damage still applies while repeat fear is blocked), so simulation should separate fear application from damage resolution.

basic_ability_2.name: Bountiful Harvest

effects.description: Active: Fiddlesticks forms a tether between itself and each nearby enemy over the cast time, then channels for up to 2 seconds to harvest their souls, revealing them in the process.

effects.context_notes: While Fiddlesticks is channeling, tethered enemies are dealt magic damage every 0.25 seconds.
While Fiddlesticks is channeling, tethered enemies are dealt magic damage every 0.25 seconds.
While Fiddlesticks is channeling, tethered enemies are dealt magic damage every 0.25 seconds.
While Fiddlesticks is channeling, tethered enemies are dealt magic damage every 0.25 seconds.
Fiddlesticks heals itself for a portion of the pre-mitigation damage dealt, modified to 45% against monsters and 15% against minions.
Fiddlesticks heals itself for a portion of the pre-mitigation damage dealt, modified to 45% against monsters and 15% against minions.
Fiddlesticks heals itself for a portion of the pre-mitigation damage dealt, modified to 45% against monsters and 15% against minions.
Fiddlesticks heals itself for a portion of the pre-mitigation damage dealt, modified to 45% against monsters and 15% against minions.

effects.description: Bountiful Harvest ends if all targets have died or broken their tethers. If the channel was not interrupted, 60% of the current cooldown is refunded.
A nearby enemy is required to cast this ability. The target does not have to be visible to be tethered by this ability.

basic_ability_2.description: Active: Fiddlesticks forms a tether between itself and each nearby enemy over the cast time, then channels for up to 2 seconds to harvest their souls, revealing them in the process.
context_notes: Source notes (summary): Spell shield will block the tether's application but not the damage of one already applied.
Manual review (2026-02-24): Bountiful Harvest is channel-gated and proximity-dependent; periodic drain ticks resolve during channel and end early if all tethers break.
Manual review (2026-02-24): Tick cadence is quarter-second periodic damage/heal while tethered, with end-of-channel cooldown refund behavior only when the channel is not interrupted.
basic_ability_2.description_source: Active: Fiddlesticks forms a tether between itself and each nearby enemy over the cast time, then channels for up to 2 seconds to harvest their souls, revealing them in the process.

basic_ability_3.name: Reap
basic_ability_3.description_source: Active:  Fiddlesticks slashes the target area, dealing magic damage and  slowing enemies hit.

effects.context_notes: Active: Fiddlesticks slashes the target location with its scythe, dealing magic damage to enemies within the area and slowing them for 1.25 seconds.
Active: Fiddlesticks slashes the target location with its scythe, dealing magic damage to enemies within the area and slowing them for 1.25 seconds.

effects.description: Enemies hit in the center of the area are also silenced for 1.25 seconds.

basic_ability_3.description: Active: Fiddlesticks slashes the target location with its scythe, dealing magic damage to enemies within the area and slowing them for 1.25 seconds.
context_notes: Source notes (summary): - Surprise Party Fiddlesticks has a special icon for this ability.
Manual review (2026-02-24): Reap is an area cast with delayed resolution at the target location; slow applies on hit and centerline targets additionally receive silence.

ultimate.name: Crowstorm

effects.context_notes: Active: Fiddlesticks channels for 1.5 seconds, then blinks to the target location upon completion with a murder of crows flying wildly around it for 5 seconds, dealing magic damage every 0.25 seconds to nearby enemies.

Active: Fiddlesticks channels for 1.5 seconds, then blinks to the target location upon completion with a murder of crows flying wildly around it for 5 seconds, dealing magic damage every 0.25 seconds to nearby enemies.

ultimate.description: Active: Fiddlesticks channels for 1.5 seconds, then blinks to the target location upon completion with a murder of crows flying wildly around it for 5 seconds, dealing magic damage every 0.25 seconds to nearby enemies.
context_notes: Source notes (summary): Roots do not interrupt the ability despite being a movement channel.
ultimate.description_source: Active: Fiddlesticks channels for 1.5 seconds, then blinks to the target location upon completion with a murder of crows flying wildly around it for 5 seconds, dealing magic damage every 0.25 seconds to nearby enemies.

notes: Generated from From Online champion payload as part of champion corpus parity coverage.
Ability execution metadata is source-derived from castTime/speed/effectRadius when present.
This baseline should be manually refined for high-impact simulation nuances before runtime scripting expansion.

schema_notes.base_by_rank: Arrays indexed by ability rank (1-based in game, 0-based in data).
schema_notes.base_by_champion_level: Arrays indexed by champion level minus one.
schema_notes.formula: Formula objects use explicit op/terms/output_stat when reliable source math is available.
schema_notes.ratio_type: Common ratio types: total_ad, bonus_ad, ap, target_max_health, target_missing_health.
schema_notes.value_percent: Percent values are represented as whole percentages unless noted otherwise.
schema_notes.basic_attack: Basic attack timing and projectile defaults are source-derived with documented fallbacks.
schema_notes.basic_attack_raw_timing_stats: Raw timing stats preserve source payload fields for auditability.
